comments.body: Try running this in the console: jh="$(readlink -f $(which java))";printf "JAVA_HOME should be set to %s\n" ${jh%/bin/java}

That worked, thank you so much @g00se! I had indeed made the wrong assumption about what JAVA_HOME had to be set to. If you turn your comment into an answer I'll accept it, otherwise I'll write my own.

answers.body: Try to install the distro's default jre and jdk like this:sudo apt install default-jre and sudo apt install default-jdk and see if that helps.

The error message is misleading because it may say JAVA_HOME environment variable is not defined correctly even in the case where the problem is that command java is not found in $JAVA_HOME/bin/java and it'll never be in case the JDK used in WSL is the Windows one. The solution I found was editing the script wsl and change the line

JAVACMD="$JAVA_HOME/bin/java"

to

JAVACMD="$JAVA_HOME/bin/java.exe"

title: mvn says "The JAVA_HOME environment variable is not defined correctly" in WSL
body:
I want to install mvn on Ubuntu 22.04 on WLS on Windows. I have tried and given up to use the windows binaries and am now trying to install both java and mvn directly on wsl.

I have installed corretto 17 as .deb with:

wget https://corretto.aws/downloads/latest/amazon-corretto-17-x64-linux-jdk.deb
sudo dpkg -i amazon-corretto-17-x64-linux-jdk.deb
sudo apt-get install -f

and maven with

wget https://dlcdn.apache.org/maven/maven-3/3.9.4/binaries/apache-maven-3.9.4-bin.tar.gz
tar -xvf apache-maven-3.9.4-bin.tar.gz
sudo mv apache-maven-3.9.4 /opt/

I've added

JAVA_HOME='/usr/bin/java'
PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH"
export PATH

M2_HOME='/opt/apache-maven-3.9.4'
PATH="$M2_HOME/bin:$PATH"
export PATH

to .profile and sourced it.

And java seems to refer to the one I just installed:

$ java -version
openjdk version "17.0.8.1" 2023-08-22 LTS
OpenJDK Runtime Environment Corretto-17.0.8.8.1 (build 17.0.8.1+8-LTS)
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Corretto-17.0.8.8.1 (build 17.0.8.1+8-LTS, mixed mode, sharing)
$ which java
/usr/bin/java
$ whereis -b java
java: /usr/bin/java /usr/share/java

similarly for mvn

$ which mvn
/opt/apache-maven-3.9.4/bin/mvn
$ whereis -b mvn
mvn: /opt/apache-maven-3.9.4/bin/mvn /mnt/c/ProgramData/chocolatey/lib/maven/apache-maven-3.8.5/bin/mvn

while a windows binary appears it is not in first place. If I execute mvn -version however, it says

$ mvn -version
The JAVA_HOME environment variable is not defined correctly,
this environment variable is needed to run this program.

relevant parts of env (Path shortened and reformatted for readability:

JAVA_HOME=/usr/bin/java
WSLENV=
PATH=/opt/apache-maven-3.9.4/bin
    :/usr/bin/java/bin
    :/usr/bin/java/bin
    :/usr/local/sbin
    :/usr/local/bin
    :/usr/sbin
    :/usr/bin
    :/usr/lib/wsl/lib
    :/mnt/c/Program Files/Amazon Corretto/jdk17.0.1_12/bin
    :/mnt/c/Program Files/Amazon Corretto/jdk11.0.8_10/bin
    :/mnt/c/ProgramData/chocolatey/lib/maven/apache-maven-3.8.5/bin

I have exported a modified PATH without the windows binaries, but the response to mvn -version stays the same.

I would like to understand what is going on here and how I can fix JAVA_HOME, or rather have mvn accept it.
